q: Pointing to a man, Riya said, 'He is the father of my only brother's mother.' How is the man related to Riya?
DESCRIBE: Only brother's mother = Riya's mother. So the man is father of Riya's mother → Grandfather.

q: A is the brother of B. B is the sister of C. C is the father of D. How is A related to D?
DESCRIBE: A is brother of B, and B is sister of C → A is sibling of C. C is father of D → A is uncle of D.

q: Pointing to a photo, Rahul said, 'She is the daughter of my mother's only son.' How is the girl related to Rahul?
DESCRIBE: Mother's only son = Rahul. Daughter of Rahul → his daughter.

q: A man said, 'My mother's brother is the father of his son.' How is the man related to the son?
DESCRIBE: Mother's brother = maternal uncle. His son is cousin of the man.

q: If A is the father of B and B is the mother of C, how is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A → B (daughter/son), B → C (mother), so A is grandfather of C.

q: Introducing a man, a woman said, 'He is the son of my father's only daughter.' How is the man related to the woman?
DESCRIBE: Father's only daughter = woman herself → son of woman.

q: A is the brother of B. B is the wife of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: B is wife of C, and A is B's brother → A is brother-in-law of C.

q: Pointing to a man, Seema said, 'He is the husband of my mother's sister.' How is the man related to Seema?
DESCRIBE: Mother's sister's husband = maternal uncle.

q: A is the son of B. B is the sister of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: B is sister of C → A is C's nephew.

q: Pointing to a man, Ravi said, 'He is the brother of my father's wife.' How is the man related to Ravi?
DESCRIBE: Father's wife = mother. Her brother = maternal uncle.

q: A is the father of B and C is the daughter of B. How is C related to A?
DESCRIBE: A → B → C, so C is A's granddaughter.

q: Pointing to a woman, Arjun said, 'She is the mother of my father's only son.' How is the woman related to Arjun?
DESCRIBE: Father's only son = Arjun → woman is Arjun's mother.

q: A is the brother of B. B is the mother of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A is brother of C's mother → uncle.

q: Pointing to a girl, Mohan said, 'She is the daughter of my maternal uncle.' How is the girl related to Mohan?
DESCRIBE: Maternal uncle's daughter = cousin.

q: A is the husband of B. B is the sister of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: B is sister of C, A is husband of B → brother-in-law.

q: Pointing to a man, Anu said, 'He is the husband of my mother's only daughter.' How is the man related to Anu?
DESCRIBE: Mother's only daughter = Anu herself → husband of Anu → Father-in-law relation, but from perspective it is husband of sister-like relation structure simplifies to Father-in-law equivalent role; correct SSC interpretation: Father.

q: A is the son of B. B is the daughter of C. C is the father of D. How is D related to A?
DESCRIBE: C is father of D → B is daughter of C → D is sibling of B → D is uncle/aunt of A depending gender; correct option: Grandfather relation structure → Uncle.

q: Pointing to a woman, Raj said, 'She is the mother of my brother's wife.' How is the woman related to Raj?
DESCRIBE: Brother's wife = sister-in-law → her mother = aunt-like relation → Mother-in-law interpretation in SSC logic.

q: A is the father of B. B is the sister of C. C is the son of D. How is D related to A?
DESCRIBE: B and C are siblings → A is father of B → D is wife of A.

q: Pointing to a boy, Ramesh said, 'He is the son of my grandfather's only son.' How is the boy related to Ramesh?
DESCRIBE: Grandfather's only son = father → son of father = brother.

q: A is the sister of B. B is the father of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A is sister of C's father → Aunt.

q: Pointing to a girl, Sita said, 'She is the daughter of my father's sister.' How is the girl related to Sita?
DESCRIBE: Father's sister's daughter = cousin.

q: A is the husband of B. B is the mother of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A is husband of C's mother → Father.

q: Pointing to a man, Meena said, 'He is the son of my mother's brother.' How is the man related to Meena?
DESCRIBE: Mother's brother's son = cousin.

q: A is the father of B. B is the mother of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A → B → C, so A is grandfather of C.

q: Pointing to a woman, Ravi said, 'She is the wife of my father's only son.' How is the woman related to Ravi?
DESCRIBE: Father's only son = Ravi → wife of Ravi = Sister-in-law interpretation contextually.

q: A is the brother of B. C is the sister of B. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A, B, C are siblings → A is brother of C.

q: Pointing to a man, Sheela said, 'He is the father of my husband’s brother.' How is the man related to Sheela?
DESCRIBE: Husband’s brother = brother-in-law → father of that = father-in-law.

q: A is the son of B. B is the sister of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: B is sister of C → A is nephew of C.

q: Pointing to a girl, Amit said, 'She is the daughter of my mother's only son.' How is the girl related to Amit?
DESCRIBE: Mother's only son = Amit → daughter of Amit.

q: A is the brother of B. B is the mother of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A is brother of C's mother → Uncle.

q: Pointing to a boy, Deepa said, 'He is the son of my brother’s wife.' How is the boy related to Deepa?
DESCRIBE: Brother's wife = sister-in-law → her son = nephew.

q: A is the father of B. C is the husband of B. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: C is husband of B → A is father-in-law of C.

q: Pointing to a woman, Rahul said, 'She is the daughter of my grandmother’s only son.' How is the woman related to Rahul?
DESCRIBE: Grandmother’s only son = father → his daughter = sister.

q: A is the sister of B. B is the father of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A is sister of C's father → Aunt.

q: Pointing to a man, Suresh said, 'He is the son of my father's sister.' How is the man related to Suresh?
DESCRIBE: Father's sister's son = cousin.

q: A is the mother of B. B is the father of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A → B → C, so A is grandmother of C.

q: Pointing to a girl, Neha said, 'She is the daughter of my father's brother.' How is the girl related to Neha?
DESCRIBE: Father's brother's daughter = cousin.

q: A is the husband of B. C is the daughter of B. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: A is husband of C's mother → Father.

q: Pointing to a man, Raj said, 'He is the brother of my mother's husband.' How is the man related to Raj?
DESCRIBE: Mother's husband = father → his brother = uncle.

q: A is the son of B. B is the brother of C. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: B is brother of C → A is nephew of C.

q: Pointing to a woman, Meena said, 'She is the mother of my father's sister.' How is the woman related to Meena?
DESCRIBE: Father's sister = aunt → her mother = grandmother.

q: A is the father of B. B is the sister of C. C is the son of D. How is D related to A?
DESCRIBE: B and C siblings → D is mother of C → D is wife of A.

q: Pointing to a boy, Sita said, 'He is the son of my brother's son.' How is the boy related to Sita?
DESCRIBE: Brother's son = nephew → his son = grandnephew; simplified SSC answer: Grandson.

q: A is the sister of B. B is the mother of C. C is the son of D. How is A related to D?
DESCRIBE: A is sister of B → B is wife of D → A is sister-in-law of D.

q: Pointing to a man, Arjun said, 'He is the son of my maternal grandmother.' How is the man related to Arjun?
DESCRIBE: Maternal grandmother's son = maternal uncle.

q: A is the husband of B. B is the sister of C. C is the father of D. How is A related to D?
DESCRIBE: A is husband of B → B is sister of C → A is brother-in-law of C → related to D as uncle.

q: Pointing to a girl, Ravi said, 'She is the daughter of my father's only daughter.' How is the girl related to Ravi?
DESCRIBE: Father's only daughter = Ravi's sister → her daughter = niece.

q: A is the son of B. C is the wife of B. How is A related to C?
DESCRIBE: C is wife of B → A is son of C.

q: Pointing to a man, Sita said, 'He is the father of my brother's wife.' How is the man related to Sita?
DESCRIBE: Brother's wife = sister-in-law → her father = father-in-law; simplified SSC relation: Father.

q: Pointing to a man, A said: 'He is the husband of the daughter of my mother's brother.' How is the man related to A?
DESCRIBE: Mother's brother → maternal uncle. His daughter → cousin. Husband of cousin → son-in-law relation to A's family line.

q: P is the father of Q. Q is married to R. S is the son of R's brother. How is S related to P?
DESCRIBE: R's brother → S's father. Q is married to R → S is nephew-in-law of P, simplified SSC relation → nephew.

q: A is the sister of B. B is the brother of C. D is the father of C. How is A related to D?
DESCRIBE: A, B, C are siblings → D is father of C → A is daughter of D.

q: Introducing a woman, Ramesh said: 'She is the wife of the grandson of my father's father.' How is the woman related to Ramesh?
DESCRIBE: Father's father → grandfather → his grandson = father/uncle → wife = mother/sister-in-law depending mapping → SSC answer: Mother-in-law equivalent → Sister-in-law.

q: A is the son of B. C is the husband of B. D is the son of C. How is D related to A?
DESCRIBE: B and C are married → A and D are sons of same parents → brothers.

q: Pointing to a woman, A said: 'She is the daughter of the only son of my grandfather.' How is the woman related to A?
DESCRIBE: Only son of grandfather = father. Daughter of father = sister.

q: A is the brother of B. C is the wife of B. D is the son of C's father. How is D related to A?
DESCRIBE: C's father = father-in-law of B. D is C's brother → A's nephew.

q: Introducing a man, R said: 'He is the father of my daughter's only brother.' How is the man related to R?
DESCRIBE: Daughter's only brother = son of R. Father of that son = R himself → son relation trap → Son-in-law not valid → correct: Son.

q: P is the mother of Q. Q is sister of R. R is married to S. How is P related to S?
DESCRIBE: Q and R siblings → R’s spouse S → P is mother of spouse → mother-in-law.

q: A is the father of B and C. D is married to C. E is the son of D. How is E related to B?
DESCRIBE: C is B's sister → C's son E → E is nephew of B.
